title: A Fresh Start for Your Career in Recruitment Consulting
description: * **Channel your drive into a role that fuels a company in rapid expansion.** * **Your effort sets the pace – fast and completely performance\-based career progression.** * **Join a business that rewards ambition and invests in your future.** What if your career moved forward exactly as fast as you’re willing to push it? What if you didn’t have to wait for someone else’s approval to take the next step? At **Access** , your results speak louder than anything else. Here, **progression isn’t about time served, it’s about performance** . If you bring energy, resilience, and hunger to succeed, there’s no ceiling on what you can achieve. Maybe you’re already in recruitment and ready for a step up. Or maybe you’re looking for a new challenge: a role where ambition, people skills, and results are truly rewarded. Either way, this is your chance to build something exciting. **What the role involves:** As a Recruitment Consultant, you’ll be backed by a leadership team dedicated to helping you grow quickly and confidently. Every day brings variety and purpose: * Building relationships with clients and candidates across the healthcare sector * Managing live vacancies from first briefing to final offer * Interviewing, qualifying, and advising candidates through each stage of the process **What’s in it for you:** * **Career Progression** \- Rise fast through a clear, merit\-based pathway into senior and leadership positions. * **Continuous Development** \- Access expert mentorship, hands\-on training, and specialist knowledge to accelerate your career. * **Recognition \& Rewards** \- Every milestone matters. Expect real recognition, added responsibility, and outstanding financial rewards. The **average total compensation for year 1 is more than €45k, and for year 2 it’s over €75k** . Of course, due to our uncapped commission structure, top performers earn much more than this from the jump. * **Leadership Academy** \- Exclusive access to our Leadership Academy for advanced training, certifications, and networking with top performers. **You’ll be a great fit if you have:** * At least 1 year of experience in a fast\-moving environment (any sector) * A proactive, ambitious mindset and the confidence to take ownership of your growth * Strong communication skills and a natural drive to succeed **Your success story could look like Rian’s:** In just **24 months** , he progressed from Recruitment Consultant **to Associate Manager** , building a high\-performing team and shaping his own leadership path. In two years, you could be his peer, and you could be telling this same story to your friends, but this time about yourself. For more information, contact César Toscano at **\+353 1 572 1161** or email your CV to **cesar.toscano@accesshealthcare.ie**

Join a business powered by purpose and a place that empowers you to design your own reinvention.

title: AI Architect
description: **Shape the Future with Dun \& Bradstreet** At Dun \& Bradstreet, we believe data has the power to create a better tomorrow. As a global leader in business decisioning data and analytics, we help companies worldwide grow, manage risk, and innovate. For over 180 years, businesses have trusted us to turn uncertainty into opportunity. We’re a diverse, global team that values creativity, collaboration, and bold ideas. Are you ready to make an impact and help shape what’s next? Join us! Explore opportunities at dnb.com/careers. We are seeking an experienced and visionary **AI Software Architect** to lead the design and delivery of enterprise\-scale AI and GenAI solutions. This role requires a strong foundation in software engineering, deep expertise in AI/ML frameworks, and a strategic mindset to architect secure, scalable, and high\-performance systems. The ideal candidate will have a proven track record of hands\-on development and architectural leadership across diverse AI domains. **What’s on Offer at D\&B Ireland** * 25 days annual leave (plus 2 paid volunteer days \& 1 paid un\-sick day) * Holiday buy \& sell (the option to buy or sell up to 5 additional days per year) * Flexible working \- hybrid model * Employee Health Insurance * Mental Health Support program * Pension Contribution * Family Friendly Leave (Maternity, Paternity, Parental, Marriage and Bereavement) * Life Assurance * Educational Assistance Program * Life\-Style Account (D\&B will match your contributions up to €40 per month and can be used to claim for a range of health\-related, leisure or lifestyle activities) ***At Dun \& Bradstreet, we are 6,000 friendly colleagues around the world waiting to meet you and give you the opportunity to grow your career.*** **As part of the RDI team, you will:** * Architect and oversee the implementation of AI/GenAI systems including LLM\-based applications, RAG pipelines, and agentic workflows. * Translate business requirements into scalable technical solutions using modern AI frameworks and cloud\-native technologies. * Lead cross\-functional engineering teams through the full SDLC—from ideation and prototyping to deployment and monitoring. * Ensure compliance with security, privacy, and responsible AI standards. * Collaborate with stakeholders across product, data science, and infrastructure to align architectural decisions with strategic goals. * Mentor engineers and contribute to the development of reusable patterns, templates, and best practices. **About you:** * Minimum **5\+ years** of professional experience in software development. * Strong proficiency in **Python** with experience in building microservices and RESTful APIs. * Experience with frameworks such as LangChain, LangGraph, RAG, Agentic * Strong prompt engineering and use of evaluation frameworks (e.g., LangSmith) * Familiarity with LLM system guardrails **for responsible AI** * Solid understanding of **CI/CD pipelines** , **DevOps practices** and **containerization** (Docker, Kubernetes). * Experience with **cloud platforms** such as **GCP** , **Azure** , or **AWS** , preferably GCP ***Architectural Leadership*** * Minimum **3\+ years** in a software architecture role, ideally focused on AI/ML systems. * Proven experience designing and deploying **enterprise\-scale AI solutions** . * Familiarity with **security architecture** , **data governance** , and **performance optimization** . **Preferred Qualifications** * Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Computer Science, Software Engineering, Artificial Intelligence, or related field. * Experience working in regulated environments or with sensitive data. * Strong communication and stakeholder engagement skills. * Experience mentoring junior engineers and leading architectural reviews. **We appreciate you may not meet all listed criteria above, but if you have the passion and eagerness to learn and grow, we want to hear from you!!** All employees and contractors working in D\&B should be aware that they have responsibilities in relation to the Company’s Business Management System. This relates to information and its security, quality, environment and health and safety both during and post\-employment with D\&B. **Dun \& Bradstreet is an Equal Opportunity Employer** \-\-\- All Dun \& Bradstreet job postings can be found at https://jobs.lever.co/dnb. Official communication from Dun \& Bradstreet will come from an email address ending in @dnb.com.

title: AWS Engineer

replace large\-scale manual operating processes with scripts and applications. * Contribute to build and deploy automation support (CI/CD) in AWS. * Develop infrastructure as code and testable infrastructure in AWS. * Develop appropriate metrics to demonstrate performance and operational efficiency by scripting and automating better value chain monitoring and chain testing. * Drive the analysis, design, and development of automation tools for configuration, provisioning, deployment, development, and operational tasks. * Work cross\-functionally with various App DevOps teams to test and drive results in an international context. * Design automated systems management solutions with self\-repair and self\-healing as the core goal. **Have You:** * Obtained a Bachelor's degree or higher in computer science, electrical engineering, or an equivalent field. * Acquired strong, hands\-on experience with Infrastructure\-as\-Code (Terraform, CloudFormation), JSON templates, and Kubernetes. **Berkley Business \& Technology is a specialist consultancy, recruiting professionals for organizations throughout Ireland, UK \& Europe; for more information go to www.berkley\-group.com.** **If you would like to discuss this vacancy further or to discuss your career options in confidence, please send your profile to jburke@berkley\-group.com**

title: Account Executive | SMB
description: Procore is growing, and our SMB team in Dublin is the driving force behind that success. While we aren't hiring for an immediate start this second, we know the best talent doesn't always wait for a job board. We’re looking to connect with "Construction\-Curious" sales pros now so that when our next hiring sprint begins, you’re already at the front of the line. This isn't a "sit back and wait for inbound" role. We’re looking for **SMB Account Executives** who have a true "Hunter" mindset balanced with a consultative approach. You’ll be the face of Procore to SMB construction firms across the region, helping them trade in their messy spreadsheets for the world’s leading construction platform. **What you'll actually do:** * Lead your own prospecting strategy: You’ll have the autonomy to identify and open your own accounts. We have a brilliant SDR team to support you, but we value AEs who take ownership of their own pipeline. * Manage the Full Cycle: You’ll own the journey from that first outreach to the final "closed\-won" celebration. * Consult, don’t just sell: You’ll become a trusted advisor in the construction world, helping our customers build safer and more efficiently. * Master the Stack: You’ll use tools like Salesforce and Gong to sharpen your approach. We’re big on continuous learning and peer\-to\-peer coaching here. **What we're looking for:** * The SaaS "It" Factor: You have experience in B2B tech sales and the resilience to thrive in a fast\-paced environment. * Growth \& Pipeline Ownership: You take pride in your numbers and are always looking for ways to exceed your goals. * Curiosity \& Impact: You’re the type of person who wants to understand how things are built and the positive impact technology can have on a traditional industry. * Dublin Based: You’re ready to join us in our Dublin office to soak up the culture, collaborate with the team, and enjoy our famous free lunches. **The Process:** If your profile looks like a fit, our recruiting team will reach out for a "Coffee Chat" screen. No pressure, no formal case studies yet \- just a chance for us to get to know you and for you to see if Procore is your next home. ***Please Note:*** *This is a "Pipeline" posting intended to identify potential candidates for future openings. By applying, you are registering your interest for upcoming roles and not a currently active vacancy. We will review all applications and contact relevant candidates as soon as a new seat launches.* ***Base Pay Range:*** *51,660\.00 \- 71,032\.50 EUR Annual* ***On Target Earning Range:*** *103,320\.00 \- 142,065 EUR Annual* This role may also be eligible for Equity Compensation and/or Bonus Incentive Compensation. Procore is committed to offering competitive, fair, and commensurate compensation. Actual compensation will be based on a candidate’s job\-related skills, experience, education or training, and location.

title: Account Executive, New Business Sales (English, French)
description: **Minimum qualifications:** * Bachelor's degree or equivalent practical experience. * 2 years of experience in new business sales and exceeding quota attainment. * Ability to communicate in English and French fluently to support client relationship management in this region. **Preferred qualifications:** * Experience in building a pipeline from scratch through strategic and tactical focus and execution. * Experience in planning, delivering, and executing digital marketing campaigns, measurement, and creative strategy. * Understanding of the online advertising industry and knowledge of the local market with insights into both digital and traditional media. * Ability to navigate ambiguity, work independently, and work in a team environment. * Excellent communication and influencing skills with the ability to pitch comprehensive advertising solutions for all clients. **About The Job** Businesses of all shapes and sizes rely on Google’s unparalleled advertising solutions to help them grow in today's dynamic marketing environment. You bring a passion for sales, knowledge of online media, and commitment to maximize customer success. You act like an owner, move with velocity through change, finding innovative and strategic ways to consistently deliver extraordinary and incremental outcomes for both Google and your customers. You build trusted relationships with customers, uncovering their business needs and translating them into powerful solutions to achieve their most ambitious goals. You achieve as a team with sellers, shape the future of advertising in the AI\-era, and make a real impact on the millions of companies and billions of users that trust Google with their most important goals. Google Customer Solutions (GCS) sales teams are trusted advisors and competitive sellers who maintain a relentless focus on customer success by bringing the best Google has to offer to small\- and medium\-sized businesses (SMBs), which are the backbone of our communities. As a member of our team, you’ll have the opportunity to work with company owners and make a real difference in their businesses by helping them grow. Together, we help shape the future of innovation for customers, partners, and sellers...and we have fun doing it. **Responsibilities** * Manage the whole business cycle for new business acquisition from early stages to closing the customers and managing the relationship for another 6 months to ensure customer satisfaction, performance, and upsell opportunities. * Prospect and build a robust pipeline of new business, working in close partnership with our sales development, onboarding, agency, and marketing teams. * Acquire high\-touch, high\-value business agreements through outbound prospecting, cold calling, consulting, educating, pitching, closing, and relationship management. * Consult with clients to achieve domestic and export online marketing and business goals, utilizing the full suite of Google’s marketing solutions (i.e., YouTube, Google Search and Google Shopping, Google Display Network, app promotion). * Deliver consistently against strategic goals while prioritizing and delivering an outstanding experience to Google's advertisers within Europe, the Middle East, and Africa (EMEA). Google is proud to be an equal opportunity workplace and is an affirmative action employer. We are committed to equal employment opportunity regardless of race, color, ancestry, religion, sex, national origin, sexual orientation, age, citizenship, marital status, disability, gender identity or Veteran status. We also consider qualified applicants regardless of criminal histories, consistent with legal requirements. See also Google's EEO Policy and EEO is the Law. If you have a disability or special need that requires accommodation, please let us know by completing our Accommodations for Applicants form .

title: Accounting Technician / PQ Management Accountant
description: **Your new company** An established semi\-state organisation at the forefront of delivering affordable housing in Ireland is expanding its Asset Management function. With a significant national development pipeline and a mission centred on creating sustainable, thriving communities, the organisation offers a dynamic environment where your work will contribute to long‑term social impact. As part of a growing team, you will collaborate across Finance, Audit, Tax, and operational business units to support the delivery of high‑quality cost‑rental housing. **Your new role** As an Accounts Technician (Part Qualified), you will report to the Revenue Manager and play a key role in management accounting, performance reporting, and financial analysis within the Cost Rental Operations team.Your responsibilities will include: * Analysing monthly financial and data reports from third‑party agents and preparing consolidated portfolio performance reports. * Assisting in the preparation of annual budgets for the cost‑rental portfolio and Asset Management department. * Monitoring and reporting on key KPIs such as occupancy, arrears, and operating expenses. * Benchmarking performance across the portfolio to identify efficiencies and opportunities for improvement. * Preparing monthly financial reports for senior management, including narrative commentary. * Managing purchase orders and invoices to ensure compliance with internal policies. * Supporting audit processes and resolving procurement and accounts payable queries. * Preparing weekly and monthly internal reports for meetings and project reviews. * Building strong working relationships with internal teams and external stakeholders. **What you'll need to succeed** * Minimum 3 years' experience in a similar accounting or finance role. * Experience working with property‑related IT systems and reporting tools. * Strong proficiency in Microsoft Excel and Word. * Previous experience as an Accounts Technician or Management Accountant in the residential rental sector is a distinct advantage. * Excellent organisational skills and strong attention to detail. * Ability to work independently, manage competing priorities, and meet deadlines. * A collaborative mindset with the ability to work cross‑functionally. * A positive, solutions‑focused attitude and a commitment to confidentiality and professionalism. * Part‑qualified or actively pursuing an accounting qualification. **What you'll get in return** You will join a rapidly expanding organisation with a meaningful mission and a strong pipeline of impactful projects. This role offers: * A permanent, full‑time position based in Dublin. * Opportunities for professional development as the organisation continues to scale. * Exposure to a wide range of financial, operational, and strategic activities. * The chance to contribute to a nationally significant programme delivering long‑term social value. **What you need to do now** If you're interested in this role, click 'apply now' to forward an up\-to\-date copy of your CV, or call us now. If this job isn't quite right for you, but you are looking for a new position, please contact us for a confidential discussion about your career. **Desired Skills and Experience** Minimum 3 years' experience in a similar accounting or finance role. Experience working with property‑related IT systems and reporting tools. Strong proficiency in Microsoft Excel and Word. Previous experience as an Accounts Technician or Management Accountant in the residential rental sector is a distinct advantage. Excellent organisational skills and strong attention to detail. Ability to work independently, manage competing priorities, and meet deadlines. A collaborative mindset with the ability to work cross‑functionally. A positive, solutions‑focused attitude and a commitment to confidentiality and professionalism. Part‑qualified or actively pursuing an accounting qualification. Hays Ireland is a trading division of Hays Specialist Recruitment (Ireland) Limited and acts as an employment agency for permanent recruitment and employment business for the supply of temporary workers. title: Activity Facilitator Permanent
description: **Details of Service** Leopardstown Park Hospital is a registered centre for the provision of services to older people, it provides Day services and Long\-Term Care, situated in South Co. Dublin. It was established in 1917 and continues to provide interdisciplinary services on a seven\-day, twenty\-four\-hour basis **.** It is currently registered for 120 long stay beds **Salary Scale for Position €36,288 \- €45,484** Reporting Relationship : The post holder is: professionally accountable to the Assistant Director of Nursing will work closely with the Occupational Therapist Manager Purpose of the Post To provide an Activities Service to residents of LPH To support the Assistant Director of Nursing and Occupational Therapist Manager and wider OT team in the professional planning, preparation, and delivery of the Activity Timetable/Programme, with a key focus on person centred experience. To foster a collaborative approach to working with other disciplines, managers, and departments. To oversee the use and management of activities resources. The Activities Facilitator plays a key role in providing the Activities Programme Principal Duties and Responsibilities The Activities Facilitator will: Provide a high level of leadership in activities delivery. Provide safe comprehensive acivities services to residents within the scheduled programme. Provide meaningful activities suitable to the individual and group function and presentation. Recognise when adaptations in activities may be required and liaise with OT dept staff in relation to same. Work closely with the other disciplines to facilitate the smooth running of activities events and programmes in conjunction with the ADON, OT Manager, OT staff and others as appropriate. The Activities Facilitator will practice nursing according to Hospital Guidelines, policies, protocols, guidelines and current relevant legislation and regulatory standards relating to Older Persons Services. Manage own workload in accordance with the needs of the post. Participate in teams as appropriate, communicating and working in cooperation with other team members. Ensure that residents/patients and others are treated with dignity and respect. Ensure the maintenance of records in accordance with LPH standards and record on E ipiCare and elsewhere as required. Adhere to and contribute to the development and maintenance of activities standards, protocols and guidelines consistent with the highest standards of care. Manage the implementation of best practice policy and procedures. Participate in clinical audit with the QPS, as required Actively manage the Activities programme and work collaboratively with the OT Department to oversee the provision of meaningful activities across the hospital. Participate in activities for all residents groups and individuals, including those with dementias, learning difficulties, physical incapacities, etc. To undertake such duties appropriate to the post as may be assigned by the Assistant Director of Nursing and/or Occupational Therapist Manager or their deputies. Health \& Safety The Activities Facilitator will: Ensure adherence to established policies and procedures e.g., health and safety, infection control, storage and use of equipment etc. Participate in completion of incident / near miss forms (NIRF). Maintain a feedback mechanism with the ADON and OT Manager Ensure adherence to department and hospital policies and guidelines in relation to the care and safety of environment and equipment supplied/used for the fulfilment of duty. Participate in risk identification and management processes. Education and Training The Activities Facilitator will: Contribute to service development through appropriate continuous education, research initiatives, keeping up to date with activities practitioners and developments and attend staff study days as considered appropriate. **Other Requirements** Show innovation and creativity. Ability to motivate self and others. Demonstrate flexibility. Ability to proactively work with other members of the team. Solution focused approach to problem solving. The above Job Specification is not intended to be a comprehensive list of all duties involved and consequently, the post holder may be required to perform other duties as appropriate to the post which may be assigned to him/her from time to time and to contribute to the development of the post while in office. Eligibility Criteria **Qualifications And/ Or Experience** Candidates must Be educated to at least QQI level V in the Care of the Older Person. Demonstrate evidence of continuing professional development at the appropriate level. Age restrictions shall only apply to a candidate where he/she is not classified as a new entrant (within the meaning of the Public Service Superannuation Act, 2004\). A candidate who is not classified as a new entrant must be under 65 ears of a e. **Leopardstown Park Hospital is an equal opportunities employer.** **Closing date for applications is Monday 13th April 2026 @ 4\.00pm**

title: Administrative Business Partner

Responsibilities** * Manage complex calendars across multiple international time zones * Coordinate domestic and international travel arrangements, itineraries, and logistics * Schedule leadership title: Agile Coach
description: Job: Agile Coach Location: North Dublin, Ireland Rate: 550\-650 per day Type: Daily rate Contract Term: 12 months\+ Working arrangement: Hybrid 2 days onsite per week. Elwood Roberts is working closely with a well\-known Irish Business who are going through a major digital transformation and are looking for a talented and experienced Agile Coach. As an Agile Coach you will play a critical role in evolving our customer's Agile journey, to increase agility and improve delivery through continuous improvement. This will be achieved by working with stakeholders across the technology department and across the wider business. In the role you will work with the technology teams to enhance the agile journey and the steps, practices and roles needed to continue to implement and embed the agile framework across the organisation. **We are looking for people with the below experience:** * Deep understanding of current Agile models aligned to emerging technologies and successful tech industries, along with the capabilities to guide and influence stakeholders and teams in building and embedding an agile framework into a complex delivery organisation. This experience to be demonstrated in at least five years in working in similar environments. * Proven experience and knowledge of successfully implementing and enhancing an agile framework for a complex technical delivery organisation. * Knowledge and experience of CI/ CD practices is essential. * Experience in successfully coaching multiple agile teams. * Proven experience facilitating planning with business and IT stakeholders. Providing direction, guidance or recommendations around agile processes and improvements. * Ensure that the agile approach aligns with business and technology trends and strategy. * Proven ability to work effectively under pressure and commitment to producing high quality outcomes to tight deadlines. * Excellent communication and documentation skills with the ability to present facts, plans, training clearly and objectively. * Strong interpersonal skills with the ability to manage effective working relationships at all levels both internally and externally. * Proven ability to implement change management and build strong relationships. * Strong commercial awareness and an understanding of financial cost models * Strong user of Atlassian Suite Tools especially JIRA and Confluence, Tempo would be desirable. This is an excellent opportunity to get the foot in the door of an industry giant. You will plan a major role in the success of the on\-going projects that plays a critical role in the companys ability to increase revenue and continue to modernise their technology. There are brilliant career opportunities on offer also.

title: Analyst
description: We’re working with a leading residential property developer in Dublin to hire an Analyst to join a high\-impact, commercially focused team. This is a unique opportunity for someone who enjoys working behind the scenes to shape senior\-level decision\-making. You’ll play a key role in producing the reports, analysis, and presentations that inform how the business evaluates its developments — past, present, and future. The role is not client\-facing but offers significant exposure to senior leadership and multiple functions across the organisation, including development, finance, and commercial teams. **The Role** * Produce high\-quality executive reports providing insights across completed, live, and pipeline developments * Deliver monthly live site updates, tracking performance, risks, and key milestones * Maintain and continuously improve a master tracker of all developments across the business * Support pipeline reporting on prospective sites and future opportunities * Conduct scenario analysis to support strategic planning and investment decisions * Compile key project reports highlighting priority developments * Create polished presentations for senior leadership to deliver to internal stakeholders and external partners * Ensure all reporting is accurate, consistent, and clearly structured **The Person** * 2–5 years’ experience in real estate, property, or a related analytical role * Strong Excel skills and experience with Power BI or similar tools * Solid understanding of the property development lifecycle * Highly detail\-oriented with strong data management capabilities * Organised, proactive, and comfortable managing multiple workstreams * Strong written and presentation skills **Why Apply?** * High visibility role with direct exposure to senior decision\-makers * Broad insight into a full development pipeline * Opportunity to work cross\-functionally in a leading Irish business * Strong platform for career progression in real estate or strategic roles **To discuss in confidence, contact Caroline via cogrady@elevatepartners.ie or apply directly via the link provided.**

title: Android Developer
description: **Role: Android Payments Developer** **Location: Dublin, Ireland** **Type: Contract** **Job Description:** Senior Android Payments Developer — POS Legacy Transformation We are seeking a Senior Android Payments Developer to lead development of card acceptance applications on POS devices and play a significant role in a legacy modernisation programme. You will design and implement robust, secure Android software for terminals (including PAX devices), work closely with QA, product and programme teams, and drive payment certification with major card schemes, **Key Responsibilities:** Design and implement Android applications for point\-of sale terminals: deliver clean, maintainable Java/Kotlin code that runs on embedded Android and AOSP\-based POS devices. Integrate with POS hardware: implement and validate drivers and SDK integrations for card readers, PIN pads, NFC/contactless, EMM readers, printers and peripherals (experience with PAX SKs and device\-specific APIs expected). Payment flow implementation: authorisation, settlement, reversal and offline\-capable flows; ensure correct handling of EMV, contactless and magnetic stripe scenarios. Support payment certification: prepare artefacts, run test suites and coordinate certification activities with card schemes and acquirers (Visa, Mastercard, American Express and others); address scheme audit findings and remediation. Contribute to legacy transformation: migrate and refactor legacy payment codebases to modern architectures (modularisation, services, secure SDKS), preserving backward compatibility and minimising customer impact. Security and compliance: Implement secure coding practices, support PCI and terminal security requirements (PCI PTS, EMV kernel considerations), and liaise with security and HSM teams on key management interraces. Performance, reliability and observability: Profile and optimise application performance, improve startup times and memory usage, and add telemetry/diagnostics to aid support and SRE teams. Automation and C/CD: Build and maintain automated build, test and release pipelines for terminal images and application packages; support automated regression and device farm test runs. Defect resolution and production support: drive root cause analysis for critical incidents, produce fixes and participate in postrelease reviews. Mentoring and collaboration: review peers' code, share knowledge across the team, and work closely with QA leads, programme managers and product owners to align delivery priorities. Primary Objectives: Deliver high\-quality Android POS applications for multiple device families (including PAX) that meet functional, security and performance targets. Successfully completed payment certification cycles with major card schemes and acquirers, achieving approval with minimal rework. Advance the legacy transformation programme by migrating key components to modern, maintainable implementations while minimising disruption to services. Reduce production defects and incident mean time to resolution through better testing, telemetry and automated checks. Ensure software fulfils compliance requirements and is supportable in live environments. **Knowledge Skills:** Strong hands\-on Android development experience (lava and Kotlin) on embedded/AOSP variants used in POS devices. Proven experience with POS device SKs and hardware integration —experience with PAX devices and SDKs highly desirable. Deep understanding of payment terminal flows, EV transaction lifecycle, contactless/NFC processing and implications for application logic. Practical familiarity with payment scheme certification processes and requirements for Visa, Mastercard, American Express (preparing logs, test cases, remediation). Awareness of industry security standards: PCI (including PCI PTS) and EMV kernel behaviours; understanding of secure key management concepts and HSM interactions. Experience with automated testing and Cl/CD for mobile/embedded applications; unit, integration, Ul automation and device farm orchestration. Proficient with modern development toolchains: Android Studio, Gradle, Git, experience with Jenkins/GitLab Cl/Azure DevOps or equivalent. Competence in debugging on physical devices, serial/USB/Bluetooth/NFC communications, and analysing logs and traces from constrained environments. Strong analytical skills, attention to detail and methodical approach to debugging and problem resolution. Effective verbal and written communication; able to prepare technical artefacts for certification, and present technical trade\-offs to product and programme stakeholders. Experience working in regulated/financial environments (fintech, payments) and in large enterprise delivery contexts. Educational Professional Requirements: Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Engineering or equivalent practical experience. Relevant certifications (Android, EMV, PCI or equivalent) are advantageous. Demonstrable track record of shipping payment terminal software and participating in card\-scheme certification or acquirer testing is highly desirable

title: Associate Architect
description: Paysafe is a leading payments platform with an extensive track record of serving merchants and consumers in the global entertainment sectors. Its core purpose is to enable businesses and consumers to connect and transact seamlessly through industry\-leading capabilities in payment processing, digital wallet, and online cash solutions. With 29 years of online payment experience, an annualized transactional volume of $152 billion in 2024, and approximately 3,300 employees located in 12\+ countries, Paysafe connects businesses and consumers across 260 payment types in 48 currencies around the world. Delivered through an integrated platform, Paysafe solutions are geared toward mobile\-initiated transactions, real\-time analytics and the convergence between brick\-and\-mortar and online payments. Further information is available at www.paysafe.com . **It starts here. Have a global impact on the world of payments.** The Financial Platforms Architecture team defines the architecture, integration patterns, and technology strategy across the company’s Finance Technology landscape. The team works closely with Finance, Accounting, Treasury, FP\&A, Risk, and Engineering stakeholders to deliver a modern, scalable, and well\-controlled set of finance platforms that support accurate financial reporting, reconciliation, treasury operations, and regulatory requirements. The Associate Architect – Financial Platforms is a hands\-on solution architecture role intended for experienced solution architects or highly seasoned senior engineers with strong enterprise system design experience. This role is suitable for someone who already demonstrates architectural judgement and is capable of owning solution design within their domain while operating within a broader architecture governance model. This role contributes directly to the design and evolution of Finance platforms, including ERP, reconciliation, treasury, reporting, and integration layers. It operates under the guidance of a Senior Architect and Dev leadership but is expected to work independently on defined problem spaces and initiatives. The role focuses on financial platforms and the post\-transaction financial lifecycle, rather than merchant\-facing or real\-time payment authorization platforms. **What Paysafe stands for:** * Being open and honest. * Keeping focused. * Operating with Courage. * Pioneering the future. Our values and culture are driven by Equality, Development, Social Responsibility and Wellbeing. If you want to find out more about life at Paysafe, check out our careers page here . **How we work:** We follow a hybrid working model, spending an average of three days per week at our office location. This will be based in our Dublin hub. The Dublin office is located in George's Quay in the heart of Dublin. **What to expect:** * Evaluate Finance and business requirements and translate them into coherent, end\-to\-end solution designs under architectural governance * Design solutions spanning Finance platforms such as ERP, reconciliation, treasury, reporting, and analytics systems * Contribute to architecture decisions across system boundaries, including data flows, integrations, and platform responsibilities * Produce clear architecture and design artefacts for new initiatives and enhancements aligned to Finance Technology standards * Act as a trusted technical point of contact for Finance Technology solution design topics * Articulate complex, data\-driven solutions to Finance, Accounting, Treasury, FP\&A, and Technology stakeholders * Collaborate with cross\-functional teams to ensure solutions support financial controls, auditability, and operational resilience * Support platform rationalisation and transformation initiatives, including consolidation of systems and reduction of technical debt * Apply and promote best practices across finance system design, integration patterns, data consistency, and architectural governance **To be successful, you will have:** * Demonstrable experience in solution or platform architecture, or equivalent senior\-level experience designing complex enterprise systems * Strong understanding of Finance Technology domains such as accounting, ledger management, reconciliation, treasury, and reporting * Experience designing and integrating enterprise platforms such as ERP, EPM/consolidation, reconciliation, and treasury systems * Hands\-on experience with enterprise integration and middleware platforms and system\-to\-system integrations * Strong understanding of financial data flows, lineage, and system\-of\-record concepts * Ability to design solutions that support data integrity, auditability, and regulatory compliance * Proven ability to work effectively with senior Finance and Technology stakeholders * Excellent written and verbal communication skills with the ability to explain technical designs in business terms **Considered an advantage:** * Experience supporting Finance Transformation or ERP modernisation initiatives * Exposure to regulatory, safeguarding, or audit requirements within financial services * Experience designing solutions that support month\-end close, reconciliation, or financial reporting processes * Familiarity with enterprise reporting, analytics, or self\-service BI patterns * Previous experience operating within a formal architecture practice or governance framework **A snippet of what you’ll get in return:** * Make your day work for you with our flexible working hours. * You decide what your holiday looks like with the option to buy or sell your holiday and carry over up to 5 days into the next year. * Start your day with a free breakfast, fresh fruit and snacks. * Spend time with those important to you with our enhanced paid family policies. * Test our products Skrill and Neteller. Upon joining we will award you £50 into each wallet. * Enjoy our discounts on memberships via vitality including, gyms, leisure centres, yoga/Pilates across the country. * Need a new Laptop or TV? We offer support purchasing Apple and LG products via Stormfront technology. * Join our six employee\-led equality communities and help foster a workplace that celebrates diversity and create opportunities to collaborate and learn. * Give back to the community with four paid charity days. * Kickstart your weekend early with our summer hours during the months of June, July and August with a 3pm finish every Friday. * Let’s not forget, we also offer: Private health insurance, dental insurance, income protection, life assurance and more. **What to expect next:** * Phone screen with Talent Acquisition. * Video interview with the Solution Architect. * Video interview with VP Technology, US Acquiring and Finance. * Final HR interview with Talent Acquisition. **Equal Employment Opportunity** Paysafe is an equal opportunity employer. We value diversity and are committed to providing a work environment of mutual respect to everyone without regard to race, color, religion, national origin, age, gender identity or expression, or any other characteristic protected by applicable laws, regulations and ordinances.

title: Associate QC
description: **Overview:** Responsible for planning, executing and documenting the qualification, calibration and routine maintenance of laboratory systems for cGMP Quality Control laboratory facilities. Responsible for ensuring that all maintenance \& calibration activities carried out by Calibration Technicians are tracked \& managed through the Computerised Maintenance Management System (CMMS) Maximo. Responsible for authoring/owning and approving Validation Life Cycle documents for computer related systems including responsibility for Data Integrity testing and business administration of Laboratory Computer Related systems. Responsible for the execution of protocols once approved. Candidate should have specific hands\-on laboratory equipment lifecycle and validation experience. **Key Responsibilities include:** \* Planning and conducting routine calibration, requalification and maintenance of laboratory equipment ensuring calibration and maintenance schedules are adhered to as per CMMS Maximo. Reviewing \& filing of QC calibration \& maintenance documentation. \* Planning, executing and documenting the qualification of new laboratory equipment in a cGMP regulated environment, including the writing of equipment validation protocols and associated summary reports. \* Conduct periodic reviews of instrument validation as part of validation life cycle. \* Alerting Quality Control Management in the event of equipment failing to meet calibration or qualification requirements and conducting impact assessments/investigations as required. \* Scheduling and coordination of equipment repairs and maintenance with vendors/contractors, while minimizing level of down\-time for lab equipment, and disruption to laboratory activities. \* Participation in technical project teams to act as a subject matter expert on instrument validation regulations and procedures. \* Where applicable, owns and project manages change controls and adherence to Change Control metrics \* Supporting and managing the addition of project components to CMMA Maximo \* Act as a key contributor and sponsor to Data Integrity Assessments for Lab systems \* Any other tasks/projects assigned as per manger's request **Knowledge and Experience:** \* Bachelor's degree in a Science related field is required. \* Minimum of 3 years of laboratory experience in a cGMP laboratory environment including exposure to analytical instrument validation and data integrity assessments. \* Detailed knowledge of the methodology, and instrumentation and analytical techniques used for biopharmaceutical testing. \* Understanding of current regulatory requirements for cGMP laboratory equipment validation and electronic record integrity. \* Demonstrated success in managing an equipment qualification or maintenance program advantageous. The successful candidates will be involved with routine activities and may be requested to support project work. For the most part project work consists of the introduction and qualification of analytical equipment for use in routine QC analysis and the candidate should have specific hands\-on laboratory equipment lifecycle and validation

title: Azure Cloud Consultant
description: **Company Description** Version 1 has celebrated over 28 years in Technology Services and continues to be trusted by global brands to deliver solutions that drive customer success. **Responsibilities** * Manage and monitor our clients Azure infrastructure to ensure high availability, performance, and security. * Design and implement Azure\-based cloud architectures with a focus on containerized workloads using Kubernetes (AKS). * Provision, configure, and maintain Azure resources and services, including virtual machines, Data Lake and synapse workspace, storage accounts, virtual networks, and security groups. * Monitor and troubleshoot Azure systems and applications, identifying and resolving performance, connectivity, and security issues. * Collaborate with cross\-functional teams such as Enterprise Architect Team and IT Operations to support the deployment and integration of Azure\-based solutions. * Lead cloud migration and modernization projects, including re\-platforming and re\-architecting applications for Azure. * Develop and maintain Infrastructure as Code (IaC) using tools like Terraform, Bicep, or ARM templates. * Implement CI/CD pipelines and DevOps practices for containerized applications. * Monitor, troubleshoot, and optimize Kubernetes clusters and Azure resources. * Ensure security, compliance, and governance across cloud environments. * Provide technical leadership and mentorship to client teams and junior consultants. * Document solutions, create runbooks, and deliver knowledge transfer sessions. **Qualifications** * Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Engineering, or related field. * 4\+ years of experience in cloud consulting or engineering roles. * Strong knowledge of Azure cloud platform, including core services, administration tasks, and resource management. * Deep understanding of Kubernetes architecture, deployment, scaling, and troubleshooting. * Proficiency in scripting and automation (e.g., PowerShell, Bash, Python). * Experience with containerization tools (Docker) and orchestration platforms (Helm, Istio, etc.). * Familiarity with CI/CD tools (e.g., Azure DevOps, GitHub Actions, Jenkins). * Excellent communication and client\-facing skills. **Additional Information** **Why Version 1?** At Version 1, we believe in providing our employees with a comprehensive benefits package that prioritises their wellbeing, professional growth, and financial stability. * Share in our success with our Quarterly Performance\-Related Profit Share Scheme, where employees collectively benefit from a share of our company's profits. * Strong Career Progression \& mentorship coaching through our Strength in Balance \& Leadership schemes with a dedicated quarterly Pathways Career Development programme. * Flexible/remote working, Version 1 is tremendously understanding of life events and people’s individual circumstances and offer flexibility to help achieve a healthy work life balance. * Financial Wellbeing initiatives including; Pension, Private Healthcare Cover, Life Assurance, Financial advice and an Employee Discount scheme. * Employee Wellbeing schemes including Gym Discounts, Bike to Work, Fitness classes, Mindfulness Workshops, Employee Assistance Programme and much more. Generous holiday allowance, enhanced maternity/paternity leave, marriage/civil partnership leave and special leave policies. * Educational assistance, incentivised certifications, and accreditations, including AWS, Microsoft, Oracle, and Red Hat. * Reward schemes including Version 1’s Annual Excellence Awards \& ‘Call\-Out’ platform. * Environment, Social and Community First initiatives allow you to get involved in local fundraising and development opportunities as part of fostering our diversity, inclusion and belonging schemes. And many more exciting benefits… drop us a note to find out more. Video links: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v\=F\_d3ELTH5zo

Pay: €12\.00\-€14\.15 per hour Education: * Leaving Certificate (preferred) Language: * English (required) Work Location: In person

title: Branch Colleague (A7)
description: **Timpson** ***‘One of the best places to work in the UK’ The Sunday Times*** **(Part of Timpson Group Limited)** Are you ready to embark on a rewarding new career? If you’ve spent time in retail or hospitality and are eager for a fresh challenge, look no further! Timpson, recognized by The Sunday Times as 'One of the Best Places to Work in the UK,' is calling for dynamic individuals to join our successful high street service team. **Position:** Branch Colleague for Timpson, a Service\-Based Retail Company **Location: Naas** **Job Type** : Permanent / Full Time **Pay** : Starting at 29536 per annum with OTE 31k **Hours** : 40 hours over 5 days, including weekends, with overtime opportunities available post\-training. **Job Description** Your day\-to\-day responsibilities will include serving customers with key cutting, watch and mobile phone repairs, engravings, shoe repairs, photo processing, dry cleaning and many other services. Lack of experience is not an issue for us, as every single colleague is given in\-depth training from their very first day. You’ll be walked through exactly how things are done, as we offer a fun, valuable learning experience that quickly teaches you the new skills you’ll need to succeed. We’ll set you on the path for a successful and rewarding career, and then show you how to excel at Timpson! **As part of your job role, you will be expected to Complete an act of kindness for customers or colleagues every day** **What We Offer:** Taking care of our colleagues is our top priority. Every year, we measure our “happiness index” to understand how our team feels and how we can improve. In addition to a basic wage, all our colleagues can earn weekly performance bonuses. Benefits include: * Free holiday homes * Achievable uncapped bonuses * Discount card (up to 90% off for colleagues, up to 25% off for friends and family) * An extra day off for your birthday * Paid day off for a child’s first day at school * Annual salary reviews * Regular social events * £1000 weekly lottery draws * Excellent promotion prospects * Ongoing skills training * £100 bonus when you get married * Mental health support, including free flu jabs and a 24\-hour helpline * … and more! **Who We’re Looking For:** We value personality over qualifications. Ideal candidates are positive, friendly, confident, and great communicators. We welcome applicants from all backgrounds, including ex\-offenders. **Who We Are:** As a family\-run business since 1865, Timpson is proud to be the largest service retailer in the UK with over 1,300 stores in the Uk and Ireland. We pride ourselves on our culture of trust, giving colleagues the freedom to excel in their roles. If you're interested in varied work and career progression, this could be the right role for you! **Next Steps:** To apply, click the button below and fill out the application form. We look forward to meeting you!

title: Brand Writer
description: ****About Remote**** Remote is solving modern organizations’ biggest challenge – navigating global employment compliantly with ease. We make it possible for businesses of all sizes to recruit, pay, and manage international teams. With our core values at heart and future focused work culture, our team works tirelessly on ambitious problems, asynchronously, around the world. You can find Remoters working from 6 different continents (Antarctica left to go!) and all of our positions are fully remote. With Innovation as one of the core values, we have built Automation and AI capabilities into the requirements for every role. We encourage every member of the Remote team to bring their talents, experiences and culture to the table to help us build the best\-in\-class HR platform. If you are energetic, curious, motivated and ambitious, be part of our world. Apply now and define the future of work! ****About Remote**** Remote is solving the biggest challenge in modern business: building and scaling global teams. We enable companies to employ anyone, anywhere compliantly, while simplifying payroll, benefits, taxes, and HR. Our mission is to create opportunity everywhere — and storytelling plays a critical role in making that real. ****What You’ll Do**** **Define and evolve Remote’s brand voice** Shape a distinctive, consistent voice that reflects Remote’s clarity, confidence, and global perspective. Establish tone and editorial standards that scale across campaigns and our website. **Own creative campaign storytelling end\-to\-end** Lead messaging and writing for creative campaigns — from big ideas and narratives to execution across channels. Develop work that is memorable, differentiated, and deeply aligned with our audience. **Elevate the Remote website** Write and optimize high\-impact website content that clearly communicates our value, simplifies complex topics, and drives conversion. Continuously refine messaging based on performance and audience insight. **Create scalable content systems** Develop repeatable formats, messaging frameworks, and modular content that can be reused and adapted across campaigns and web experiences. **Turn attention into action** Craft content that doesn’t just inform — it moves people. Help transform visitors into customers, and customers into advocates through compelling, conversion\-oriented storytelling. **Experiment and push the brand forward** Explore new formats, channels, and creative approaches. Bring fresh, modern storytelling techniques into Remote’s brand ecosystem. **Scale with AI (thoughtfully)** Leverage AI to increase speed, consistency, and creative ambition — while maintaining a strong editorial point of view and human quality bar. **Collaborate across Remote** Partner with marketing, product, design, and leadership to bring sharp, differentiated perspectives to life. Translate complex global employment topics into clear, engaging narratives. **Raise the bar for brand writing** Set a high standard for craft, clarity, and creativity. Continuously improve how we tell Remote’s story across every touchpoint. ****What You Bring**** * Proven experience writing high\-quality brand and marketing content across formats — especially creative campaigns and web * Exceptional writing and editing skills, with a strong sense of voice, tone, and narrative structure * Strong editorial judgment, including knowing what not to create * Experience translating complex topics into clear, engaging, and accessible content * Deep understanding of modern content patterns (clarity, structure, flow, and conversion principles) * A clear perspective on how AI can enhance content creation * Track record of driving measurable impact through content (brand, engagement, and conversion) * Ability to collaborate cross\-functionally and influence stakeholders * Experience in HR tech, SaaS, or fintech — or strong curiosity about the future of work and global employment **Why This Role Matters** This role sits at the heart of how Remote shows up in the world. You’ll help define how we tell our story — making complex global employment feel simple, human, and compelling. Your work will shape first impressions, drive growth, and build a brand that companies trust as they expand globally. **Application process** * Interview with recruiter * Interview with future manager * Interview with team members * Bar Raiser Interview * Prior employment verification check Remote's Total Rewards philosophy is to ensure fair, unbiased compensation and fair equity pay along with competitive benefits in all locations in which we operate. We do not agree to or encourage cheap\-labor practices and therefore we ensure to pay above in\-location rates. We hope to inspire other companies to support global talent\-hiring and bring local wealth to developing countries. At first glance our salary bands seem quite wide \- here is some context. At Remote we have international operations and a globally distributed workforce. We use geo ranges to consider geographic pay differentials as part of our global compensation strategy to remain competitive in various markets while we hiring globally. Our salary ranges are determined by role, level and location, and our job titles may span more than one career level. The actual base pay for the successful candidate in this role is dependent upon many factors such as location, transferable or job\-related skills, work experience, relevant training, business needs, and market demands. The base salary range may be subject to change. *At Remote, we foster internal mobility as a key element of our culture of employee growth and development, supported by a compensation philosophy that guarantees pay equity and fairness. Therefore, all compensation changes associated with an internal move will be reviewed by the Total Rewards \& People Enablement team on a case by case basis.* The annual salary range for this full\-time position is $90,000—$120,000 USD ****Benefits**** Our full benefits \& perks are explained in our handbook at remote.com/r/benefits. As a global company, each country works differently, but some benefits/perks are for all Remoters: * work from anywhere * flexible paid time off * flexible working hours (we are async) * 16 weeks paid parental leave * mental health support services * stock options * learning budget * home office budget \& IT equipment * budget for local in\-person social events or co\-working spaces ****How You’ll Plan Your Day (and Life)**** We work async at Remote which means you can plan your schedule around your life (and not around meetings). Read more at remote.com/async. You will be empowered to take ownership and be proactive. When in doubt you will default to action instead of waiting. Your **life\-work balance** is important and you will be encouraged to put yourself and your family first, and fit work around your needs. If that sounds like something you want, apply now! ****How To Apply**** * Please fill out the form below and upload your CV with a PDF format. * We kindly ask you to submit your application and CV in English, as this is the standardised language we use here at Remote. * If you don’t have an up to date CV but you are still interested in talking to us, please feel free to add a copy of your LinkedIn profile instead. Not only do we encourage folks from all ethnic groups, genders, sexuality, age, abilities, disability status and any other under\-represented group to apply, but we prioritize a sense of belonging. We have 4 ERGs (Women, Disability, Queer, Minorities in Tech) who meet regularly with the People team. During your interviews and beyond, we ask \& encourage anybody who needs an accommodation to request one from their recruiter. We will ask you to voluntarily tell us your pronouns at interview stage, and you will have the option to answer our anonymous demographic questionnaire when you apply below. As an equal employment opportunity employer it’s important to us that our workforce reflects people of all backgrounds, identities, and experiences and this data will help us to stay accountable. We thank you for providing this data, if you chose to. At Remote, we embrace AI as a valuable tool while prioritizing human creativity and authenticity. We look forward to meeting candidates who balance innovation with genuine expertise and experience. To learn more about Remote's AI guidelines check see here. *Please note we accept applications on an ongoing basis.*

title: Building Management Systems Engineer
description: **Junior BMS Engineer** Are you a technically minded graduate, junior engineer or electrician ready to kickstart your career in Building Management Systems (BMS)? An exciting opportunity has opened up for a Junior BMS Engineer to join a highly respected BMS integrator working on some of the most innovative commercial, pharmaceutical and mission\-critical projects across Ireland. **Dublin, Ireland** * €40,000 \- €45,000 * Yearly Bonus * Company Vehicle * Full Training and Development * Private Healthcare **Responsibilities:** * Assist in the design, commissioning and support of BMS systems across a variety of cutting\-edge projects. * Work closely with senior engineers and project managers to gain hands\-on experience with systems like Trend, Tridium or Cylon. * Get involved in both on\-site and office\-based work, no two days are the same. * Learn the full project lifecycle from specification and design, through to programming, installation and commissioning. **Experience Required:** * Degree or diploma in electrical engineering, building services, mechatronics or a related field. * Electrically Qualified. * A passion for automation, controls or energy\-efficient building systems * Excellent problem\-solving skills and eagerness to learn * Ideally some exposure to HVAC or BMS systems (through a placement, internship, or coursework). **What’s On Offer:** * Competitive salary and benefits package * Structured training and development plan * Opportunity to gain certifications and hands\-on experience with industry\-leading BMS technologies * Career progression with a clear pathway toward project engineering and senior roles * Collaborative team environment with supportive mentors For more information please contact: luke.alliband@clear\-er.com

title: Business Analyst
description: Role overview * As a Business Analyst and Consultant, within the Banking Consulting Group, the person will be responsible to engage with clients across the stages of the Project Lifecycle – Requirements gathering, Mapping processes, Solution architecture \& Design through to development, UAT and deployment. This person should be a subject matter expert in Payments, fraud \& SCA domain with 6 \- 10 years of experience, having worked on large regulatory projects. This person will work closely with Client Business/IT team \& internal development. Role Responsibilities * Interacting with business owners, product teams, product vendors, other applications owners, external customers and internal customers to identify requirements and deliver those within stipulated timelines. * Lead deep technical analysis of PSR fraud and SCA obligations, translating regulatory requirements into detailed functional, non‑functional and control specifications across authentication, fraud detection, refunds and exemptions, leading to clear, regulator‑defensible technical requirements aligned to regulatory intent. * Own technical solution design support, working with architecture, security, fraud and payments teams to define system behaviors, integrations, data flows and edge cases (e.g. real‑time payments, risk‑based SCA) so to enable technically sound, build‑ready designs with reduced delivery and compliance risk. * Support build, testing and BAU readiness, ensuring technical requirements are traceable to controls, scenarios, MI and operational tooling. So that, implemented solutions operate effectively in BAU with evidence suitable for audit and RCSA. * Provide content leadership in working with the clients in defining solutions to their business requirements. * Interacting with business stakeholders for requirements gathering, analysis, documentation etc. * Work with the delivery / implementation teams to provide domain inputs and to ensure delivery and implementation of the proposed solution. * Interacting with the technical team to ensure that the requirements are clear and well understood. * Review test strategy \& test specifications, assist in functional testing and provide UAT/BAT support * 8\+ years of Industry experience, having worked in Tier\-1 IT organizations in BFSI domain with a focus on Payments Domain experience in Low Value Payments, High Value Payments, Cross Border Payments and key regulations \& associated IT impacts, affecting the Payments market, such as Single Euro Payments Area (SEPA), Faster Payments, Payment Services Directive etc. * Should be able to able to play catalyst role between business and IT Skills: * 8\+ years of Payments experience * Expertise in Fraud and SCA * Experience of Business Analysis of Payments solutions * Good communication skills with ability to lead domain discussions with BFSI customers

title: Business Analyst
description: **Auxilia Group Recruitment is hiring an experienced Business Analyst, Programme Operations Support \& Delivery on behalf of our client, a well‑established organisation operating within a regulated health insurance environment.** \*\*Please note that due to the collaborative nature of this role, the successful candidate must be onsite in the Dublin office 4 days per week, with the option to work remotely 1 day per week\*\* **About the Role:** Our client is currently mid\-programme on a major digital transformation replacing its legacy health insurance system and introducing Oracle Health Insurance, NetSuite finance/CRM and member/provider portal systems, targeting go\-live Q4 2026\. The **Business Analyst** plays a key operational and project role supporting the General Manager, Deputy General Manager and Programme Project Manager in programme delivery, documentation, testing, training, data migration and process improvement across the programme. **Key Responsibilities** **Programme \& Vendor Session Support** * Attend key vendor and programme sessions alongside the leadership team \- capturing what is proposed, flagging impacts and surfacing alternatives for General Manager and Deputy General Manager consideration * Review vendor functional design documents ahead of leadership sign\-off \- identifying implications, open items and decisions required * Maintain requirements traceability and open decisions log throughout the programme \- keeping it current as sessions and sign\-offs progress **Testing \& Quality Assurance** * Develop, document, and maintain test scripts * Execute system and user acceptance testing (UAT) * Support defect tracking, resolution, and retesting * Provide test training and guidance to stakeholders **Training \& Support** * Design and deliver training sessions for systems and processes * Develop training materials, including user guides and manuals * Act as a point of contact for user support and queries **Documentation** * Draft and maintain policies and procedures * Create clear, structured process documentation * Ensure documentation aligns with business and compliance requirements **Data \& Systems Support** * Work with Excel and other tools to prepare and structure data for database input * Assist in data validation, cleansing and migration activities * Support system configuration and data\-related tasks **Data Migration Support** * Assess and resolve substantive data gaps with vendor and legacy system owner (as relevant) * Support Data Migration strategy including reconciliations and optimisations at each stage * Manage data migration readiness; including finance DM reconciliation **Project Involvement** * Attend project meetings and contribute to discussions * Capture requirements, actions, and decisions * Identify shortcomings, document and address with Deputy General Manager or General Manager or Project Manager ahead of the next meeting * Follow up on assigned tasks and ensure timely completion **Stakeholder Coordination** * Collaborate with business users, IT teams, and project stakeholders * Communicate updates, risks, and issues effectively * Ensure alignment between business needs and system functionality **Skills \& Experience** * Experienced Business Analyst on systems integration and deployment programmes \- regulated environment is ideal * Experience with testing methodologies and tools, UAT planning and test script design; co\-ordinating business\-led testing across multiple systems * Strong process documentation and analytical review skills \- comfortable working through vendor design documents and identifying relevant impacts * Advanced skills in Excel and data handling; experience preparing structured data for migration or system input * Strong communication and stakeholder management skills; able to work alongside senior business leadership and hold their own in vendor sessions * Experience in training design and delivery; able to create and optimise user guides, process documentation and training materials for go\-live (not from scratch) **Desirable** * Health insurance, private medical insurance or membership management experience * Familiarity with Oracle, NetSuite, Sage or equivalent ERP / health insurance platform * Irish public sector, mutual or society experience * IIBA certification (CBAP or equivalent) **Engagement** Reporting to the Project Manager, this contract is being offered on a 6\-month basis. Please note that due to the collaborative nature of this role, you **must** be available onsite in Dublin 4 days per week with the option to work remotely 1 day per week.

title: Business Analyst - Payments
description: Business Analyst \- Payments Day rate contract: 6month rolling contract Onsite requirements – 3 days onsite in Dublin Specialism: PSD2, Open Banking **Payments Business Analyst / Open Banking Consultant** We are seeking an experienced Business Analyst with strong expertise in European Payments, PSD2, and Open Banking to support key regulatory and payments transformation initiatives. Key responsibilities include: * Leading business and technical analysis across payments, fraud, authentication, and open finance domains * Translating regulatory and business requirements into epics, user stories, and functional specifications * Conducting gap and impact analysis on existing systems and processes * Defining business, data, and control requirements aligned to compliance and audit standards * Collaborating with architecture, security, and technology teams to deliver compliant customer journeys and payment solutions * Supporting development and testing teams throughout delivery lifecycle * Ensuring smooth transition to BAU with appropriate controls, monitoring, and documentation * Acting as the key liaison between business and technical stakeholders Requirements: * Strong understanding of European payments landscape and regulatory frameworks * Proven experience with PSD2 and Open Banking initiatives * Experience working across payments processing, fraud prevention, and authentication projects * Strong stakeholder management and requirements gathering skills * Experience working in regulated financial services environments This is a day rate contracting role. Please apply if you have the relevant European Payments experience and are available for contracting opportunities in Dublin.

title: Business Intelligence Developer

title: Business Internship
description: Optum is a global organization that delivers care, aided by technology to help millions of people live healthier lives. The work you do with our team will directly improve health outcomes by connecting people with the care, pharmacy benefits, data and resources they need to feel their best. Here, you will find a culture guided by diversity and inclusion, talented peers, comprehensive benefits and career development opportunities. Come make an impact on the communities we serve as you help us advance health equity on a global scale. Join us to start **Caring. Connecting. Growing together.** The business summer internship is targeted at students working towards their graduate degree in a business or science or related discipline. Other degrees will be considered when accompanied by an aptitude or interest in business practices. The role of the business intern will be to sit within one of our business teams and learn and participate in the analysis, evaluation and implementation of business decisions. **To that end, the candidate may have experience in any of the following:** * Analytical and information gathering skills * Ability to evaluate and prioritize extensive detailed data * Excellent verbal and written communication skills, conveying complex or technical information in a manner that others can understand * Ability to understand and interpret complex information * Experience successfully managing multiple requests, assessing priorities and achieving solutions under deadlines The candidate will be expected to have a basic, structured and standard approach to work. Be capable of performing routine or structured work and be able to respond to routine or standard requests. They will be expected to depend on fellow team members for instruction, guidance or direction. In return they will gain experience of business processes in a complex environment, how to prepare and present complex information in a fashion that is understandable and actionable. They will experience the value of working as a team member and gain insights into team dynamics. You will be rewarded and recognized for your performance in an environment that will challenge you and give you clear direction on what it takes to succeed in your role, as well as providing development for other roles you may be interested in. **Required Skills \& Qualifications:** * Currently working towards a graduate degree in business or science related disciplines * Demonstrated proficiency in Microsoft Office, including Word, Excel, and PowerPoint * Demonstrated ability to collect, measure, organize, and analyze data **Soft Skills:** * Proven ability to learn new technologies quickly * Proven ability to be professional, service oriented, proactive, and flexible * Demonstrated interpersonal and team skills * Demonstrated excellent verbal and written communication, with the ability to clearly explain complex or technical information **Please note you must currently be eligible to work and remain indefinitely without any restrictions in the country to which you are making an application. Proof will be required to support your application.** *At UnitedHealth Group, our mission is to help people live healthier lives and make the health system work better for everyone. We believe everyone\-of every race, gender, sexuality, age, location and income\-deserves the opportunity to live their healthiest life. Today, however, there are still far too many barriers to good health which are disproportionately experienced by people of color, historically marginalized groups and those with lower incomes. We are committed to mitigating our impact on the environment and enabling and delivering equitable care that addresses health disparities and improves health outcomes \- an enterprise priority reflected in our mission.* *Diversity creates a healthier atmosphere: Optum is an Equal Employment Opportunity employer and all qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to gender, civil status, family status, sexual orientation, disability, religion, age, race, and membership of the Traveller community, or any other characteristic protected by law. Optum is a drug\-free workplace. © 2026 Optum Services (Ireland) Limited. All rights reserved.* \#BBMEMEA
